articleTitle: A systematic review and meta-analysis on the risk of migraine in patients with allergic rhinitis.
takeaways: This systematic review and meta-analysis found that patients with allergic rhinitis have a significantly increased risk of developing migraines compared to those without allergic rhinitis
The research analyzed multiple observational studies to establish a consistent association between these two common chronic conditions that both significantly impact quality of life
The findings suggest that managing allergic rhinitis symptoms may be an important consideration for migraine prevention strategies in susceptible individuals

Healthcare providers should be aware of this connection when treating patients who present with either allergic rhinitis or migraines, as addressing both conditions simultaneously may improve overall outcomes
